page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 dedicated virtual CPUs, mapped from 40 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 320.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 107964.98 and a single-core score of 2693.39, placing both in the top 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with read-only small block speeds reaching 5084.96 GB/sec and large block write speeds at 123.81 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 77.46 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 25033.19.

While the server demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving throughput and ray tracing, other workloads fall into the average tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression operate within the middle 50% of benchmarks. LLM inference speeds are also moderate, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model reaching 112.62 tokens per second. Given the lack of local SSD storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance is suited for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, large-scale web serving, and database operations that rely on external storage attachments.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 40 physical cores with hyper-threading and provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and efficient database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 77.46 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks and LLM inference speeds remain in the average tier. This configuration is suited for enterprise web serving, database management, and distributed multi-threaded workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 80 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, suited for database operations and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 features 80 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 320 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
tagline: An 80-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory allocation for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
320.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
High-throughput capability for database and Redis operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for enterprise web serving and distributed computing
